origin: Composition table derived 2026-07-06 from ROSTER.md locked kit (11 roles) + parity rule (Heretics ≤ Loyalists at start). Updated 2026-07-18 to include L7 Sanctioned Psyker at ≥7p.
notes: TODO(heresy-spec): Composition drafted in 2026-07-06 session, not playtested. Updated 2026-07-18 to add Sanctioned Psyker at 7p+ (optional, max 1 per game).

hardRules.non_citizen_roles_unique: All roles except imperial-citizen are unique per game (no duplicate Interrogators, etc.).
hardRules.citizen_repeats: imperial-citizen appears N times to fill remaining slots after unique roles are assigned.
hardRules.priest_rationale: Priest claim target must exist whenever Heretic Priest is in the game. Priest ships at 5p; Heretic Priest ships at 6p+.
hardRules.heretic_priest_rationale: Needs Priest claim in game AND ≥1 other Heretic for cover.
hardRules.recruiter_rationale: Catalyst carrier required for v1 conversion win path.
hardRules.conspirator_rationale: Forgery needs ≥10 living players for density to matter.
hardRules.sanctioned_psyker_rationale: L7 Sanctioned Psyker ships at ≥7p (locked 2026-07-18). Optional, max 1 per game. At 7p replaces one Citizen slot via fallback priority.
hardRules.parity_friendly_start: Heretic count ≤ Loyalist count at game start (parity win rule).

fallbackRule: If player count not in table: select role subset by descending priority [sanctioned-psyker, priest, interrogator, chirurgeon, novice-psychic, arbitrator, murderer, heretic-priest, saboteur, recruiter, conspirator], then fill rest with imperial-citizen. Citizen goes last; non-Citizen roles go in priority order. Sanctioned Psyker is preferred over a duplicate Citizen when player count is 7p.
